summary: I reviewed the dark-mode diff against `guides/backend-guide.md` and `guides/ui-style-guide.md` and wrote a `needs_work` verdict with two minor findings. The implementation is strongly guide-aligned: the backend adds no misplaced business logic (only a trivial `ThemeHelper#dark_theme?` presentation helper — correctly not a service — with no controller/model/callback/job changes, plus Minitest helper and system tests); the UI centralizes neutrals as semantic CSS custom-property tokens with a class-based `@custom-variant dark`, keeps status colors as a single source of truth in `StatusHelper::TONE_CLASSES` using the guide's exact dark pattern, preserves the status word (color never alone), and updates the guide in the same PR per the CLAUDE.md mandate. The two minor deviations: **F1** — `_flash.html.erb`'s neutral branch still hard-codes raw grays (`bg-gray-50 text-gray-700 ring-gray-600/20`) on a line this PR edited, violating the guide's own "never write raw grays in a view" rule (and contradicting the prior iteration's "zero raw grays" claim); and **F2** — the toggle's `focus-visible:outline-*` differs from the guide's literally-named `ring` focus token, though I flagged it as borderline since the outline pattern already pre-exists on `main` in `phases/show.html.erb`, correcting the earlier verdict's overstated consistency claim.
